_comment: Every number the cost engine uses that is not a user input or a live price lives here, named, with a plain-English description surfaced in the UI drill-down. Change values here, never inline in the engine.

dev_requests_per_month.description: Fixed development-environment traffic floor: builders testing by hand and automated checks, independent of production scale.

uat_traffic_fraction.description: Acceptance-testing traffic as a share of production traffic.

tokens_per_gb.description: Approximate text volume in one gigabyte of typical business documents, used to size knowledge-base indexing work.

corpus_amortization_months.description: One-time cost of indexing the initial document collection is spread over this many months.

dev_corpus_fraction.description: Development uses a small sample of the document collection.

uat_corpus_fraction.description: Acceptance testing uses a representative sample of the document collection.

search_partition_gb.description: Usable document capacity assumed per managed search unit.

vs_gb_per_unit.description: Usable document capacity assumed per Databricks search index unit.

vs_dbu_per_hour.description: Compute draw assumed for one Databricks search index unit.

serving_dbu_per_unit_hour.description: Compute draw assumed for one Databricks model serving concurrency unit.

peak_factor.description: Peak traffic assumed as a multiple of the monthly average rate.

request_latency_seconds.description: Average time one request occupies capacity end to end.

concurrency_per_serving_unit.description: Simultaneous requests one serving unit is assumed to handle.

pods_per_node.description: Application copies packed onto one Kubernetes machine.

active_hours_per_month.description: Assumed working-hours usage for capacity that switches off when idle (prototype tier).

hours_per_month.description: Average hours in a calendar month for always-on capacity.

ci_runs_per_month.description: How often the development quality-check suite runs (roughly every workday).

releases_per_month.description: How often a release candidate goes through acceptance-stage regression checks.

judge_rubric_tokens.description: Grading-instructions overhead added to each AI-graded review.

judge_output_tokens.description: Length of each AI-graded review verdict.

eval_harness_dbu_per_run.description: Compute for one run of the automated (code-based) quality-check suite.

base_trace_kb.description: Baseline activity-record size written for every request.

step_trace_kb.description: Additional activity-record size per assistant step, including its inputs and outputs. Recording every step is what makes step-by-step review possible.

judged_payload_kb.description: Extra record size kept for requests selected for AI-graded review.

lakebase_cu_per_tier_base.description: Baseline Databricks operational-database capacity for per-user working state.

memory_users_per_capacity_unit.description: Extra database capacity is added for every block of this many monthly users.

postgres_storage_gb.description: Provisioned database storage for per-user working state.

prototype.label: Departmental prototype
prototype.description: Prove the idea with a small group. Efficient AI model, capacity that switches off when idle, automated code-based checks only - no AI-graded reviews.
prototype.rigor_locked_reason: Prototype keeps checking costs at zero: automated code-based checks run, but no AI-graded reviews.

production.label: Production standard
production.description: Serve real users. Capacity scales with load, and a sample of live answers gets an AI-graded review on top of code-based checks on everything.

fiduciary.label: Fiduciary-grade compliance
fiduciary.description: For decisions you must be able to defend. Highly available capacity, full activity records kept for a year, and every step of every answer gets an AI-graded review.
fiduciary.rigor_locked_reason: Compliance requires reviewing every answer, so review coverage is fixed at 100%.
